requirements.evidence: android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSTagPill.kt:28-44 composes the molecule through LSPill, LSIcon, and LSText, and host validation passed detekt, compileDebugKotlin, and test.

android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSFilterChip.kt:33-49 resolves selected vs unselected chip styling through resolveFilterChipStyle(selected), and host validation passed the Android unit suite.

android/app/src/androidTest/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSSuggestionChipUiTest.kt exists for the one-tap contract. The host still has no connected device, so execution remains an environment note rather than a code defect in this remediation round.

android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSWeatherBadge.kt:39-42 routes the badge label through ContentColor.Primary instead of the weather-condition foreground resolved by PillSemanticsTypes.kt. The icon and border use weather styling, but the label text does not.
requirements.remediation: Route the badge label through the condition-specific weather foreground path and add test coverage that fails if the label falls back to generic primary content color.

requirements.evidence: android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/PillSemanticsTypes.kt:153-205 resolves six distinct weather badge tint values and matching icons, and host validation passed the Android unit suite.
The four Android molecule files remain free of Color(0xFF) literals, and the grep gate stayed clean during host validation.
android/app/src/debug/java/com/laneshadow/sandbox/stories/molecules/LSPillSemanticsStory.kt still contains 16 pill-semantics story IDs, which satisfies the required story count.

android/app/src/androidTest/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSFilterChipUiTest.kt exists for the exact-once toggle contract. As with AC-3, host execution remains blocked by the lack of a connected device rather than by a code defect uncovered in this review round.

findings.evidence: The badge label is rendered with ContentColor.Primary even though WeatherCondition.resolveWeatherBadgeStyle() already resolves the condition-specific weather foreground. This violates AC-4's required weather-default foreground contract for the label.
findings.fix: Use the condition-derived weather foreground for the badge label and keep the icon/border/label aligned on the same weather token family.

findings.evidence: Current tests stop at the style resolver and tint uniqueness. They do not exercise the composable wiring strongly enough to catch the incorrect label color path in LSWeatherBadge itself.
findings.fix: Add focused test coverage that fails when LSWeatherBadge routes its label through a generic content color instead of the weather foreground.

summary: NEEDS_FIXES: Android's pill family is close, but LSWeatherBadge still renders its label with a generic primary content color instead of the resolved weather foreground token. The badge tests also need stronger coverage so this composable-level regression fails immediately.
